topic: Deep-review of Phase 015 save-flow planner-first trim (packet 015) plus cross-packet residual sweep for 012/013/014 closures, v3.4.1.0 changelog accuracy, and dependabot remediation

dimensions.focus: Load-bearing preservation diff (atomic-index-memory.ts + create-record.ts + content-router.ts switch + thin-continuity-record.ts bit-level check)
Fallback equivalence — plannerMode:full-auto reaches pre-015 pipeline exactly
Flag wire completeness — SPECKIT_ROUTER_TIER3_ENABLED, SPECKIT_QUALITY_AUTO_FIX, SPECKIT_RECONSOLIDATION_ENABLED, SPECKIT_POST_INSERT_ENRICHMENT_ENABLED all wired through
Follow-up API integrity — refreshGraphMetadata + reindexSpecDocs + runEnrichmentBackfill in mcp_server/api/indexing.ts are real implementations, not stubs
Planner response schema + blocker/advisory classification — structural checks as blockers, score-heavy as advisories
Eight-category routing bit-level preservation — routeAs categories unchanged, only Tier 3 default trigger altered
Dead-branch audit — F026-style phantom-render-without-persist patterns, unused imports, unreachable branches
Test coverage gap sweep — every M2-M4 code change has tests covering both planner-first and fallback paths
Docs vs runtime drift — /memory:save command card, ENV_REFERENCE.md, AGENTS.md, system-spec-kit/SKILL.md, spec.md + plan.md + decision-record.md accurately describe runtime behaviour
Cross-packet residual sweep + v3.4.1.0 changelog accuracy check + final convergence synthesis

severityConvention.P0: production-breaking regression, load-bearing preservation violation, fallback divergence, security
severityConvention.P1: quality/correctness issue, doc-runtime drift, missing test coverage, dead flag/branch
